Bug fix Cause known · surface known · narrow, one-file fix · classifier confidence 0.95

Stop the Goal Card from leaking raw markdown syntax onto the one above-the-fold surface a first reader sees.

The job statement and the 3–5 L2 assertions are emitted through escape() only, so authored inline markdown survives to the screen as literal source. The fix routes that text through the recipe sections’ existing _md_to_html path so it resolves to clean, formatted prose.

In scope

The two Goal Card emit sites in renderer.py — the job statement and each L2 assertion — rendered through the shared inline-markdown instance, with no new renderer and no change to the thin-spine DOM.

Out of scope

The sibling _first_sentence abbreviation-truncation defect (e.g. e.g. / v2.). Different root cause, tracked as its own bug.

The leak, and the fix that stops it

A scoped, known-cause display defect: the Goal Card prints literal markdown syntax instead of prose.

The defect is data-dependent and surfaces at two emit sites in renderer.py, both fed by the pure heuristics in goal_card.py. The cure reuses the shared _md_to_html instance, adds no second renderer, and keeps each assertion one contiguous <li>.

FR-001

Render the job statement through the shared markdown path

The Goal Card job statement is rendered through the same inline-markdown path the recipe sections already use (_md_to_html), so **bold**, `code`, and _emphasis_ resolve to formatted HTML instead of literal syntax.

Note: Reuse the existing shared markdown instance; do not add a second renderer.

FR-002

Resolve each L2 assertion the same way, in one contiguous list item

Each L2 assertion on the Goal Card resolves inline markdown the same way, while staying a single contiguous <li> with no fragmenting spans.

Note: The “no markdown wrapper” comment is the bug, not the contract.

What the fix must leave untouched

The change is narrow and contained; its blast radius must be zero outside the leak.

Two guardrails define “contained”: the fix cannot disturb the thin-spine DOM contract the comment-and-version layer depends on, and it must be literally invisible wherever there is no markdown to repair.

FR-003

Inline-only conversion — no new DOM anchors

The fix introduces no element id= and no data-block-anchor anywhere on the Goal Card, preserving the thin-spine DOM contract the comment-and-version layer anchors against.

Note: A markdown renderer that emits header ids would violate this — inline-only conversion is required.

FR-004

Byte-identical output where there is nothing to fix

A render whose source contains no inline markdown produces byte-identical Goal Card output to today, so the change is invisible except where it fixes the leak.

Note: Protects the existing golden snapshots for markdown-free fixtures.

How we prove it’s fixed

Done means observable on both sides of the defect: markdown-bearing sources read as clean prose, and markdown-free sources are provably unchanged.

Three checks close it — a zero-stray-syntax assertion, a DOM-contract grep, and a golden-snapshot diff.

SC-001

Zero literal markdown survives on a markdown-bearing fixture

The Goal Card of a source containing **bold**, `code`, and _emphasis_ renders formatted text with zero literal markdown characters visible.

How verified: Render a fixture carrying inline markdown; assert no stray asterisk / backtick / underscore in the Goal Card HTML text.

SC-002

The thin-spine DOM contract holds

No id= and no data-block-anchor appear anywhere in the regenerated Goal Card markup.

How verified: Grep the rendered Goal Card section.

SC-003

A markdown-free card is byte-for-byte unchanged

A markdown-free source renders a byte-identical Goal Card to the pre-fix output.

How verified: Golden-snapshot diff on an emphasis-free fixture.

Refined requirements · family bug_fix · goal e2e-bug-fix
source d280875c5ff1
